evals.name: repeated-procedure-becomes-skill
evals.prompt: Here is a transcript of a coding session I just finished (attached as session-deploy-procedure.md). Run a learning-loop reflection on it: produce the findings report, then assume I approve every finding and scaffold them. Save the findings report as report.md and any artifacts you create into the outputs directory. Do not pause to ask me for approval.
evals.expected_output: A findings report that recommends creating a portable skill for the repeated deploy procedure and cites the recurrence as evidence.
assertions: Recommends creating a SKILL for the repeated multi-step deploy procedure
Cites recurrence as evidence (the deploy steps were re-derived/repeated, or noted as happening multiple times per week)
Creates a portable SKILL.md with name and description frontmatter
Lists the actual deploy steps (build, migrate, deploy staging, smoke-test, promote) rather than a vague summary

evals.name: repeated-correction-becomes-instruction
evals.prompt: Here is a transcript of a coding session I just finished (attached as session-repeated-correction.md). Run a learning-loop reflection on it: produce the findings report, then assume I approve every finding and scaffold them. Save the findings report as report.md and any artifacts you create into the outputs directory. Do not pause to ask me for approval.
evals.expected_output: A findings report that recommends an AGENTS.md rule requiring tests before commits and identifies that the user corrected this 3 times.
assertions: Recommends an AGENTS.md instruction requiring tests before committing
Identifies the recurrence (the user corrected the same behavior about 3 times)
Notes that CI is the portable option for deterministic enforcement

evals.name: preference-and-delegation
evals.prompt: Here is a transcript of a coding session I just finished (attached as session-preference-and-delegation.md). Run a learning-loop reflection on it: produce the findings report, then assume I approve every finding and scaffold them. Write any memory files (and the MEMORY.md pointer) into an outputs/memory/ subdirectory, save the findings report as report.md, and save any other artifacts into the outputs directory. Do not pause to ask me for approval.
evals.expected_output: A findings report plus a written memory entry capturing the pnpm/strict-mode standing rules (correct frontmatter + MEMORY.md pointer), and a recommendation for a subagent to handle the recurring flaky-test investigation.
assertions: Writes a memory entry file capturing the standing preferences (pnpm over npm, TypeScript strict mode)
The memory entry has valid frontmatter with name, description, and metadata.type fields
The memory entry type is 'user' or 'feedback' (a preference / standing rule)
Adds a one-line pointer for the new memory entry to a MEMORY.md file
Recommends a SUBAGENT/agent for the recurring flaky-test investigation
